grouped_hits.title: Parkinson Matters.
grouped_hits.study_type: Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
matched_sentences: The report indicates that mortality associated with Parkinson's disease and related disorders increased substantially between 2001 and 2014.

Recent epidemiological observations have drawn attention to the rapid rise in the burden caused by Parkinson's disease over the past years, emphasizing that Parkinson's disease is a matter of serious concern for our future generations.

Furthermore, patients with Parkinson's disease and related disorders were found to die at an advanced age, and the majority die in a care home or hospital, despite a preponderant preference for many patients and their families to spend their last days at home.

grouped_hits.title: Inpatient Mortality in Parkinson's Disease.
matched_sentences: Although a majority of the American public prefer to die at home, a large percentage of Parkinson's disease patients die in acute care hospitals.
We examine trends in the clinical and demographic characteristics of Parkinson's disease patients who die in a hospital to identify populations potentially vulnerable to unwanted inpatient mortality.

Patients with Parkinson's disease admitted to a hospital from 2002-2016 were identified from the National Inpatient Sample (n = 710,013) along with their associated clinical and demographic characteristics.
